finding: Scaling the zero-shot LM from 35M to 650M collapses the AlphaMissense gap from +0.117 to +0.012 (near-parity), and ESM-650M BEATS AlphaMissense on 2/7 proteins (MSH2, PROC). The earlier 'supervised beats zero-shot on clinical pathogenicity' was a MODEL-SIZE artifact at 35M, NOT a fundamental regime property. Largest per-protein lifts: MYOC 0.531->0.970, MECP2 0.728->0.937, SLC4A1 0.848->0.904.
caveat: AlphaMissense retains a home-field advantage (its training likely overlaps these clinical labels), so a zero-shot LM reaching parity DESPITE that is the notable result. PROC stays hard for both (0.559/0.523) — a genuinely low-signal protein, not a model-size issue.
